label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the story carefully to figure out the answer to the question about fitting a working telephone booth. The story starts with a man from the telephone department getting off a bus and going to a tea stall. It mentions that a year ago the phone line was installed, and six months after that, men from the public works department put up the phone booth. Then, days later, a painter spent a whole day painting the words on it, including the misspelled "allowded" instead of "allowed". The question is asking what is true about the process of fitting a working telephone booth. Let's look at the options: Option A: "was a process that had already taken 12 months" - In the story, the phone line was installed a year (12 months) ago, and six months after the line was installed, the booth was put up. So the total time from installation of the line to the booth being put up was 1.5 years, not 12 months by itself. The process of installing the booth itself is part of the 6 months after the line was installed. So A is not exactly correct. Option B: "was in the charge of the headman." - The headman is mentioned in the story, but there's no indication that he was in charge of the installation of the booth. He just receives the phone number and looks at it reverently. So B is not correct. Option C: "should have been finished more than a year ago" - The phone line was installed a year ago, and the booth was put up six months after that. So the booth was put up 1.5 years after the initial planning, perhaps. However, the story doesn't say that the booth should have been finished more than a year ago; it just describes the timeline of events. So this may not be correct either. Option D: "was an artistic challenge for a local painter" - The story mentions that a painter spent a whole day painting the words on the booth, including the misspelled word.
